summary: Steganographic backdoor: base64 payload hidden in logo.png executed via new Function on require
details: On require('vite-plugin-logo'), the package's top-level code walks up to 5 parent directories searching for public/assets/logo.png, reads the file, locates the marker `__VITE_ASSET_CACHE_v1__`, base64-decodes the bytes following the marker, and executes the decoded string via `new Function('require', code)(require)` (index.js:30-31). The exec is wrapped in a silent try/catch. A Vite plugin has no legitimate reason to scan parent directories for an image asset and execute bytes embedded in it; this is a hidden code-execution channel disguised as asset-cache handling. Any developer who installs this plugin into a project that contains (or later receives) a tainted logo.png will run attacker-controlled code with full Node `require` access at import time. The package presents itself as a benign Vite logo helper under the generic author identity 'Vite Community', consistent with namespace-luring within the popular Vite plugin ecosystem.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
